title: pilosella lactucella subsp. bergidensis
intro:
Pilosella lactucella subsp. bergidensis, also known as a rare subspecies of the Pilosella lactucella plant, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to certain regions of Europe.

This plant is believed to possess medicinal properties due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pilosella lactucella subsp. bergidensis include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage.

In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosella lactucella subsp. bergidensis is often prepared in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
